# Proverbs 20

## Verses (30)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | Wine makes men foolish, and strong drink makes men come to blows; and whoever comes into error through these is not wise. | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | The wrath of a king is like the loud cry of a lion: he who makes him angry does wrong against himself. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | It is an honour for a man to keep from fighting, but the foolish are ever at war. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | The hater of work will not do his ploughing because of the winter; so at the time of grain-cutting he will be requesting food and will get nothing. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | The purpose in the heart of a man is like deep water, but a man of good sense will get it out. | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | Most men make no secret of their kind acts: but where is a man of good faith to be seen? | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | An upright man goes on in his righteousness: happy are his children after him! | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | A king on the seat of judging puts to flight all evil with his eyes. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | Who is able to say, I have made my heart clean, I am free from my sin? | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | Unequal weights and unequal measures, they are all disgusting to the Lord. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | Even a child may be judged by his doings, if his work is free from sin and if it is right. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | The hearing ear and the seeing eye are equally the Lord's work. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | Do not be a lover of sleep, or you will become poor: keep your eyes open, and you will have bread enough. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | A poor thing, a poor thing, says he who is giving money for goods: but when he has gone on his way, then he makes clear his pride in what he has got. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | There is gold and a store of corals: but the lips of knowledge are a jewel of great price. | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | Take a man's clothing if he makes himself responsible for a strange man, and get an undertaking from him who gives his word for strange men. | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | Bread of deceit is sweet to a man; but after, his mouth will be full of sand. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | Every purpose is put into effect by wise help: and by wise guiding make war. | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | He who goes about talking of the business of others gives away secrets: so have nothing to do with him whose lips are open wide. | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | If anyone puts a curse on his father or his mother, his light will be put out in the blackest night. | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | A heritage may be got quickly at first, but the end of it will not be a blessing. | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | Do not say, I will give punishment for evil: go on waiting for the Lord, and he will be your saviour. | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | Unequal weights are disgusting to the Lord, and false scales are not good. | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | A man's steps are of the Lord; how then may a man have knowledge of his way? | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | It is a danger to a man to say without thought, It is holy, and, after taking his oaths, to be questioning if it is necessary to keep them. | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | A wise king puts evil-doers to flight, and makes their evil-doing come back on them. | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | The Lord keeps watch over the spirit of man, searching all the deepest parts of the body. | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
| [28](BBE.md#v28) | Mercy and good faith keep the king safe, and the seat of his power is based on upright acts. | [WEB](WEB.md#v28) · [KJV](KJV.md#v28) · [ASV](ASV.md#v28) |
| [29](BBE.md#v29) | The glory of young men is their strength, and the honour of old men is their grey hairs. | [WEB](WEB.md#v29) · [KJV](KJV.md#v29) · [ASV](ASV.md#v29) |
| [30](BBE.md#v30) | By the wounds of the rod evil is taken away, and blows make clean the deepest parts of the body. | [WEB](WEB.md#v30) · [KJV](KJV.md#v30) · [ASV](ASV.md#v30) |
